title: TechData
description: Schema for a single tech (basic attack, support tech, or ultimate) in the Remaster Engine. Per §7.3 of the design document, a tech is a typed data resource with schema-validated JSON loading. The augmentation model from §3.5 means a tech is never a standalone action; it is always a modification to a base's basic attack line or to another tech's effect. Minimal version covering the PoC's data needs per §15.4 Step 2.

id.description: Lowercase snake_case identifier. Matches the filename in data/techs/.

display_name.description: Display name shown in UI. Mirrors §3.7 locked per-base tech display names.

tier.description: Magic tier slot the tech occupies (1..8). Per §3.4 / §3.8, the tier determines when the slot is unlocked by level-based progression.

element.description: Color element. Closed set of 6 per §3.4 locked design.

cost_mp.description: MP cost to execute. Default 0 because the basic attack line (per §3.5) is free — no MP economy for the basic attack. Support techs and tier-8 ultimates have non-zero cost.

base_damage_multiplier.description: Multiplier applied to the base damage roll before augmentations and the resistance chart. Per §7.3, this is the §3.5 'basic attack line' baseline. Augmentations modify this in place via DAMAGE_MULTIPLIER_BONUS entries.

target_scope.description: Whom the tech targets. Closed enum matching the §7.3 TargetScope enum. FREE_SLOT is the open grid slot from §3.6 (later §3.6-replacement: level-based progression per locked design, but the enum value remains for compat).

slot_kind.description: How the tech was sourced. BASIC_LINE is a base's locked basic attack. SUPPORT_FIXED is a support's locked assignment. SUPPORT_PLAYER_CHOICE is the rare open grid slot (per §3.6, the level-based progression has replaced the open grid in the locked redesign, but the enum value remains).

kind.description: What the augmentation does. Per §7.3 the six kinds are: pre/post-damage status, damage multiplier bonus, on-hit chain, MP discount, self buff.

chance.description: Probability the augmentation applies. Per §7.3, the §6.7 BattleRNG is the source of this roll.

status.description: Status effect to apply (when kind is *_STATUS). e.g. 'sleep', 'poison'.

value.description: Numeric effect of the augmentation. For DAMAGE_MULTIPLIER_BONUS this is the bonus added to base_damage_multiplier; for MP_DISCOUNT this is the MP saved; for SELF_BUFF this is the buff magnitude.

turns.description: Duration in turns for status / self-buff augmentations.

augmentations.description: List of TechAugmentation entries per §7.3. Empty for the basic attack line (locked, no augmentations at tier 1). Future support techs populate this.

kind.description: What the effect does. DAMAGE is the canonical basic-attack effect; HEAL is restoration; APPLY_STATUS / REMOVE_STATUS are status manipulators; MODIFY_FIELD is the §7.4 element grid manipulation.

magnitude.description: Magnitude of the effect. Damage/heal/buff amount.

effects.description: List of TechEffect entries. Per §7.3, a tech is composed of zero or more effects. The basic attack line has exactly one DAMAGE effect; support techs may add heal/status/modify-field effects on top.
